description: Durable desired-state declaration for all active Hermes cron jobs. Source of truth for what should be running. If Hermes cron is lost on restart, use this file to re-create jobs.

jobs.name: argus-daily-brief
jobs.description: DVS Argus daily intelligence brief — Tavily + RSS + Reddit + X signals
jobs.note: Replaces all prior argus-daily-brief.sh and v2/v3 variants. Canonical runner uses --send-telegram.

jobs.name: athena-synthesis
jobs.description: DVS Athena daily knowledge synthesis — reads Argus inbox, produces structured knowledge
jobs.note: Depends on Argus inbox file being present. athena-synthesis.py handles missing deps gracefully.

orphaned_jobs.name: athena-weekly-synthesis
orphaned_jobs.reason: No wiki log reference; not actively used; weekly cadence not confirmed

orphaned_jobs.name: wiki-daily-backup
orphaned_jobs.reason: No wiki log reference; purpose unknown

persistence_risk.restart_survival: UNKNOWN — cron jobs NOT in git, NOT in a named volume
persistence_risk.mitigation: This file serves as durable desired-state. After any Hermes/cron reset, use this file to re-create jobs via: hermes cron edit <id> --prompt '<full command>' --cron '<schedule>'
